---
title: "Lesson 6: Reflecting & Exploring AI Careers"
crossref:
  lst-title: "🎯 Checkpoint 1."
  lst-labels: alpha a
format: live-html
sidebar: lesson-6
---

**This module is designed to be used in conjunction with the resources located in the Lesson 6 facilitator's guide.**

::: {.gray-container}

**Lesson Objective:** In this final lesson, you will reflect on all five workshops, explore how AI is used in real-world careers, and rotate through five hands-on stations that revisit classification, regression, and AI prompting skills.

**Materials Needed:**
- Your computer
- A web browser (Chrome, Firefox, or Safari)
- A packet (from your teacher)
- Pencil
:::

## 🎉 Welcome Back, Scientists — This is Our Final Workshop!

Over the past five workshops, you've built a real set of skills:

 - In workshop #1, we learned about introductory statistics and the Python programming language.
 - In workshop #2, we learned how to use an AI classification model and how Smokey Buoy uses this kind of model to check for new test pads.
 - In workshop #3, we learned how to use an AI regression model to predict nitrate levels from RGB color values on test strips.
 - In workshop #4, we learned about physical computing — how tiny computers, other components, and computer code can come together to make really great things.
 - In workshop #5, we used AI and prompt engineering to ask questions of and analyze real water quality data from Smokey Buoy.

## Today: Five Stations!

Today's lesson is organized into **five stations**. You'll rotate through each one with your group:

| Station | Topic |
|---|---|
| 🔁 **Station 1** | Review: Workshops |
| 💬 **Station 2** | Review: AI Prompting |
| 🍅 **Station 3** | Review: Classification |
| 📈 **Station 4** | Review: Regression |
| 🏢 **Station 5** | AI Careers |

Your teacher will let you know when to rotate. Let's get started!
